title: Least Privilege Editing
description: Practice sudo for one command in an isolated Docker training environment. Complete the briefing tasks and create the completion marker.
safetyNotes: Commands run only inside the lab container — not on your host.
Do not paste destructive commands (rm -rf /, mkfs, dd to disks) even inside the lab.
tasks: Open the lab terminal (session user is already logged in)
Read the briefing in ~/README.txt about sudo for one command
Use Check in the lab panel — the lab completes when objectives pass

objectivesPublic.label: Finish the exercise
objectivesPublic.text: Apply what you learned about sudo for one command and create the completion marker.

hints: Focus on sudo for one command.
Use ls, cat, and touch — no root privileges required.

postLabReview.summary: You practiced sudo for one command in a safe container.
skillsPracticed: Least Privilege Editing reinforces everyday sysadmin skills.
Always verify files with ls -l after creating markers.
Repeat the lab to build muscle memory.
